Title: e-Derma compulsory for new Subsection 44(6) applications from June 15 – IRB
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: All new applications for approval under Subsection 44(6) of the Income Tax Act 1967 involving institutions or organisations (IOs) must be submitted through the e-Derma system from June 15, 2026.
In a statement today, the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia (IRB) said the move is part of its efforts to expand digital service delivery and improve the efficiency of reviewing and approving tax exemption applications.
According to IRB, the e-Derma system allows applications to be submitted online at any time without the need to visit an IRB office or submit physical documents.
However, new applications under Subsection 44(6) involving funds or Institutions/Organisations/Funds (IOTs) represented by tax representatives or tax agents must continue to be submitted manually together with supporting documents for the time being.
The board said the e-Derma system would be extended to this category in a later phase.
In addition to new applications, the platform also facilitates requests for extensions of approval periods, submission of documents such as Audited Financial Statements and Self-Review Forms, as well as amendments to IOT-related information.
Users can access these services through the IRB and MyTax portals, with a user manual available to assist with the submission process.
IRB said the digitalisation of tax-related services is expected to improve service delivery through faster processing and approvals, better document management, and more convenient access for IOTs.
The initiative is also in line with the board's ongoing efforts to strengthen a more efficient, transparent and customer-friendly digital tax ecosystem.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Army tightens border control, records operation success almost daily
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The army has continued to tighten control along the country's borders to curb smuggling activities, recording successful arrests and seizures almost every day.
Army Chief Gen Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman said security along the border had never been relaxed, with personnel remaining on high alert at all times to ensure the country's sovereignty is safeguarded.
He said the TDM had also identified locations frequently used as traditional routes by smugglers and intensified surveillance in those areas.
"All planned border control operations throughout the country have so far been carried out smoothly," he told reporters after officiating the Thoriq Bin Ziyad Mosque upgrading project and Forum Perdana Programme held in conjunction with the 93rd Army Day celebration at Kem Perdana Sungai Besi here today.
He said among the key operational focuses were efforts to combat the smuggling of controlled goods such as fuel and cooking oil, as well as other items including livestock.
He said one of the latest successes involved the seizure of 115 sacks of rice smuggled from Thailand in three separate incidents around Sungai Golok, Kelantan, last Monday.
In addition, he said the procurement tender for four utility combat helicopters for the Army Air Corps (PUTD) had yet to be finalised, while several other asset acquisitions remained at the preliminary evaluation stage.
It was reported that the
PUTD
is scheduled to receive eight helicopters, which are expected to arrive and enter service in stages beginning in August next year.
The procurement involves four AW149 helicopters under a 15-year lease arrangement, while another four utility combat helicopters will be leased for five years through an open tender process.
Meanwhile, he said the Thoriq Bin Ziyad Mosque at the Sungai Besi camp, which has been upgraded at a cost of RM5.3 million, can now accommodate up to 1,700 worshippers compared with 1,200 previously.
The project also involved the construction of a new main gate, covered walkways, replacement of the mosque's dome, and the provision of a parking area that also serves as a multipurpose space.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Galatek Technologies eyes US$100mil investment in Penang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Galatek Technologies plans to invest up to US$100 million in Penang in five years following the opening of its first manufacturing, assembly and delivery facility in Malaysia.
The facility represents Galatek's first manufacturing footprint in Malaysia and will serve as a key production and delivery centre supporting customers across Southeast Asia and international markets.
The investment forms part of the company's broader regional expansion strategy and its long-term commitment to developing local manufacturing capabilities, talent and supply chain partnerships.
Backed by investors including Granite Asia, Galatek invested US$2 million (RM9.4 million) in its initial Malaysian operations.
The company plans to increase its total investment in Penang to US$100 million within the next five years as it expands production capacity and operational capabilities.
The facility will focus on advanced equipment manufacturing, system integration, assembly, testing and delivery activities, supporting industries including semiconductors, advanced electronics and life sciences.
Through its Penang operations, Galatek aims to strengthen local supplier participation while increasing the localisation of manufacturing and procurement activities.
Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said Galatek's commitment to further investment and workforce expansion in Penang reflected the company's confidence in the state's future and reinforced Penang's reputation as the "Silicon Valley of the East".
"It demonstrates global confidence in Penang's capabilities not only as a manufacturing base, but also as a centre for innovation, technology development and high-value production," he said in a statement.
The establishment of the facility comes amid continued growth in demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment and advanced production technologies.
The investment also comes as Malaysia seeks to attract more high-technology manufacturing projects, supported by its established semiconductor ecosystem and skilled workforce.
Galatek develops technologies that integrate advanced optics, precision motion control, artificial intelligence and automation to improve manufacturing efficiency and process accuracy.
The company maintains operations across Asia, Europe and the US, with Malaysia serving as a key location for its regional production and delivery activities.
Galatek chief executive officer David Tian said Malaysia offers a strong foundation for advanced manufacturing, supported by a capable workforce, established semiconductor supply chains and a conducive business environment.
He said Penang was selected as a strategic location for the company's regional manufacturing and delivery operations because of its industry expertise and connectivity to global markets.
"The opening of this facility marks an important step in Galatek's long-term development strategy in Southeast Asia.
"Beyond manufacturing, we aim to build local engineering capabilities, expand collaboration with Malaysian suppliers and contribute to the continued growth of the country's high-technology industries," he said.
The Penang facility is expected to create between 20 and 30 jobs in 2026 across engineering, operations, procurement, sales and technical support functions.
Over the next five years, the company plans to expand its Malaysian workforce to 100 employees as production activities scale up.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN chairman Samsuri to attend 'Reset' movement convention tomorrow
Article:
KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar will attend the "Reset" Malaysia Convention tomorrow in Tanah Merah.
Samsuri, who is also Pas vice-president, will join other Pas leaders at the event that will be officiated by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
The event, where former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin is expected to unveil the movement's direction, will be held at the Kota Harmoni Expo Site.
It was reported today that about
10,000 people
are expected to attend the event
Hamzah, the former opposition leader, launched the movement on Feb 14, a day after Bersatu sacked him amid a leadership tussle with party president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.
The Larut member of parliament has since been linked to plans to take over a new party with several other sacked Bersatu leaders, including Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah (Indera Mahkota), Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal (Machang) and Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan (Tasek Gelugor).
Hadi had earlier today acknowledged that Pas held a
meeting with Hamzah
in Rusila yesterday.
However, he did not elaborate on the outcome of the discussion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Welfare Dept seeks family of girl under care since 2023
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: The Welfare Department is seeking the family of a six-year-old girl who has been under its care since March 2023.
Afifah Hilya Nafisah, born on July 17, 2020, has been placed in a welfare institution since March 8, 2023, after she was suspected to be a victim of physical abuse by her guardian.
In a statement, the department said efforts to locate the child's biological mother, identified as Ida Rohana, an Indonesian, have so far been unsuccessful despite a media appeal issued on March 10, 2023.
The department said it is issuing a second public appeal to trace the child's next of kin to facilitate welfare assessments and long-term planning for her future.
"The department always prioritises the care of children in a safe and stable family environment.
"Placement in a welfare institution is a temporary measure and a last resort while awaiting the best solution for the child's welfare and future," it said.
The department urged the child's mother or any family members related to her to come forward and contact the Seberang Prai Tengah district Welfare Office.
The department said information and cooperation from the child's relatives are important to enable both the court and the department to make the best decision regarding her welfare, safety and future.
"If the mother or family members are unwilling or unable to assume responsibility for the child, they should formally inform the department to assist in planning a suitable permanent placement," it added.
This is the second public appeal issued by the department in its effort to locate the child's family.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Genting Plantations unit unveils RM80bil Johor Tech Smart City
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Genting Plantations Bhd's subsidiary Genting Property Sdn Bhd has launched a large-scale development combining advanced agriculture, technology and smart infrastructure to support future-ready food systems and urban growth.
Spanning 930.8 hectares, the Johor Tech Smart City, project is expected to generate more than RM80 billion in gross development value (GDV) and create over 10,000 jobs.
Genting Property said the project will integrate agricultural technology (AgTech), artificial intelligence, sustainable infrastructure and smart urban planning into a single ecosystem.
The development is positioned as a model for next-generation cities, bringing together food security, innovation and economic growth within one integrated environment.
The company said the project is designed to support emerging industries while creating a platform for future economies built around technology, sustainability and resilience.
The development combines Genting Property's planning expertise, the AgTech capabilities of ACGT Sdn Bhd, and the technological strengths of its strategic partners.
A 202.3-hectare AgTech Campus will be dedicated to smart farming initiatives aimed at strengthening food security through several core components.
These include Seeds, which focuses on crop and seed research and development, breeding programmes and quality agricultural inputs, as well as Superfarms, which supports smart cultivation and scalable crop production.
Meanwhile, a 688-hectare Knowledge AI Campus will anchor the digital economy and talent development agenda.
It will feature a 430-acre innovation hub based on Huawei's blueprint for advanced infrastructure and digital ecosystems.
The launch was officiated by Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, Genting Bhd executive chairman Tan Sri Lim Kok Thay and Genting Plantations chief executive and executive director Datuk Indera Lim Keong Hui.
Onn Hafiz said the development reflects the progress of Johor's ongoing efforts to strengthen a competitive, innovation-driven and sustainable economic ecosystem.
"Through close cooperation between the state government, industry players and relevant agencies, Johor continues to facilitate quality investments, nurture skilled talent and create wider economic opportunities for the people.
"Johor Tech Smart City demonstrates what can be achieved through strong public-private collaboration and a shared long-term commitment to Johor's sustainable development," he said.
Meanwhile, executive director Lim said by combining Genting Property's township development expertise with ACGT's AgTech innovations and global partnerships, the group is confident the project will significantly support Johor's economic growth, innovation ecosystem and future readiness.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court dismisses Indira Gandhi's suit on unilateral conversion laws
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today dismissed a lawsuit by M. Indira Gandhi and 13 others seeking to invalidate Islamic laws in several states that allegedly allow the unilateral conversion of minors to Islam.
Judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman said the plaintiffs had failed to show they were directly affected by the disputed provisions.
The lawsuit, filed in March 2023, sought declarations that provisions in Islamic laws in Johor, Perlis, Melaka, Kedah, Negri Sembilan, Perak and the Federal Territories, which allegedly permit the unilateral conversion of minors to Islam, are unconstitutional and contrary to Article 12(4) of the Federal Constitution.
The plaintiffs also sought declarations that unilateral conversions carried out after the Federal Court's landmark 2018 ruling in Indira Gandhi's case were invalid.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three Indonesian children reunited with families after being found abandoned
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Three children aged between 1 amd 3 who were under the care of the Welfare Department after being found abandoned have been reunited with their families in Indonesia.
Efforts were undertaken to trace their relatives in Indonesia after they were found, to ensure the children's right to grow up in a family environment could be fulfilled.
The exercise was successfully carried out through close cooperation among the department, the Indonesian consulate-general and the Immigration Department.
Following the identification of the children's next of kin, all necessary documentation and repatriation arrangements were coordinated by the relevant authorities.
Penang Social Development, Welfare and Non-Islamic Religious Affairs Committee chairman Lim Siew Khim said the successful reunification reflected the strength of cross-agency cooperation and bilateral ties between Malaysia and Indonesia in safeguarding children's welfare.
"Every child has the right to receive love, protection and the opportunity to grow up in a safe family environment.
"We hope their return home today will mark the beginning of a better chapter in their lives with their respective families," she said.
Lim also thanked Indonesian consular officer Nelvy Meilia Syah, Penang Welfare Department director Rozita Ibrahim, the Immigration Department and the Welfare Department's Children Division personnel for their commitment and cooperation throughout the process.
She said the Penang government remained committed to safeguarding the welfare, protection and rights of every child.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SD Property's Elmina Lakeside Mall wins global retail award
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd's Elmina Lakeside Mall has won the World Gold Award under the 'Retail' category at the 2026 FIABCI World Prix d'Excellence Awards in Vienna, Austria.
The award, organised by the Fédération Internationale des Administrateurs de Biens-Conselis Immobiliers (FIABCI), recognises real estate projects globally based on factors including functionality, impact and value creation, beyond design and scale.
SD Property group managing director and chief executive officer Datuk Seri Azmir Merican said the recognition reflected the group's approach towards integrated township development.
"Winning this award validates our approach to creating integrated developments that deliver value beyond individual assets.
"Elmina Lakeside Mall was conceived as part of a broader township ecosystem, bringing together retail, recreation, community spaces and nature within a connected environment that supports how people live, work and interact," he said.
Built around retail, community and nature
Located in the City of Elmina, Elmina Lakeside Mall was designed around the concept of "mall in a park, park in a mall", combining retail, leisure and community spaces within a walkable environment.
The 21-hectare open-air retail development comprises nine blocks of single- and double-storey buildings, with about 214,000 sq ft of net lettable retail space.
It serves as the commercial centre of the 142-hectare Elmina City Centre and reached full occupancy within eight months of its soft opening in August 2024.
The mall currently serves a catchment area of about one million residents.
Apart from retail offerings, the development includes indoor and outdoor event spaces, a lakeside promenade and connectivity to the adjoining 14-hectare Urban Park.
The development achieved GreenRE Silver certification, while its sales gallery received GreenRE Platinum certification.
SD Property said the mall incorporates features including solar-ready roofing, LED lighting, water-efficient fittings, electric vehicle charging bays and bicycle-friendly facilities.
The World Gold Award follows Elmina Lakeside Mall's Gold Award win in the 'Retail' category at the FIABCI Malaysia Property Awards 2025, which allowed it to represent Malaysia at the global awards.
"International recognition such as this reinforces the strength of our township development model and our commitment to creating long-term value," Azmir said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] MIPP picks Pas, snubs BN in polls alliance gamble
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) has decided to align itself with Pas ahead of the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan elections, rejecting overtures from Barisan Nasional (BN) despite direct engagement from its leadership.
MIPP president S. Punitha said the decision followed a meeting with Pas vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in Kuala Lumpur yesterday, where both sides discussed future political cooperation and the party's role within Perikatan Nasional (PN).
Speaking in an interview with the New Straits Times, Punitha said Pas had offered MIPP a platform to strengthen Indian representation while respecting the distinct identities of both parties.
He said MIPP and Pas shared a common objective of rebuilding PN's appeal and positioning the coalition as a stronger alternative for voters.
Punitha said discussions also covered seat allocations for future elections, with MIPP expected to contest four Johor seats under the arrangement.
He said the party was drawn to Pas' commitment to equal opportunities, including improved access to higher education and greater support for the B40 community.
On MIPP's decision to reject BN, Punitha said the coalition had been in power for too long and had failed to adequately address longstanding concerns affecting the Indian community.
He also criticised Pakatan Harapan (PH) for allegedly failing to deliver on promises made during the last general election.
Punitha said MIPP's primary objective remained championing issues affecting the Indian community, particularly access to education, social mobility and welfare, while continuing to represent Malaysians of all backgrounds.
He said Pas, under Samsuri's leadership, was undergoing a transformation aimed at broadening its appeal beyond traditional support bases, adding that the focus should now be on a "people's wave" rather than narratives surrounding a "green wave".
MIPP's central committee is expected to meet on Sunday to formally endorse the party's future
political direction
, although Punitha said the consensus to work with Pas had already been reached at the leadership level.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vietnam police bust group planning large-scale online scam centre
Article:
HANOI: Vietnamese police have busted a group suspected of trying to establish a large-scale online scam centre in the country, authorities said on Friday, as criminal networks spread their operations across Southeast Asia.
Police in Phu Tho province uncovered and disrupted a transnational group linked to online fraud syndicates operating in Cambodia, preventing it from setting up what they described as a major scam hub in Vietnam, the Ministry of Public Security said in a statement.
Four people were arrested, including a Chinese national and three Vietnamese, according to the statement.
Investigators said the group had rented multiple resorts, farmstays and villas in Hanoi, Lao Cai and Phu Tho to house dozens of people as part of their preparations, adding that many of them had previously worked at scam centres in Cambodia.
Police also seized dozens of computers, hundreds of mobile phones and internet devices allegedly used for online fraud, saying the site was close to becoming operational.
The raid "prevented the formation of a large-scale transnational high-tech fraud centre within Vietnam," and also helped safeguard national security and protect people's assets, the ministry said.
Speaking at a virtual press briefing on Wednesday, FBI Co-Deputy Director Andrew Bailey described scam compounds as among "the most significant threats facing the world today", warning that their impact across Southeast Asia was "growing at an exponential rate."
Bailey said the operations are run by sophisticated transnational networks that move people, money and technology across borders, exploiting weak governance and emerging tools to expand globally.
Amnesty International said ‌in a report on Monday that dozens of suspected ​global scam compounds in Cambodia are still in operation despite a months-long crackdown by authorities.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cop acquitted and discharged of RM41,000 bribery charges
Article:
TAWAU: A policeman was today acquitted and discharged by the Sessions Court here of three charges of soliciting and accepting RM41,000 in bribes.
Inspector Saharulzaman Alizaman, 42, was cleared of all three charges at the close of the prosecution's case.
Judge Ahmad Faizhad Yahaya, in delivering his ruling, said the prosecution's evidence was insufficient to support allegations that the accused had solicited and received money for corrupt purposes.
In his judgment, he said the court found serious inconsistencies between the testimony of the complainant and that of an eyewitness, giving rise to reasonable doubt over the prosecution's case.
He added that the investigating officer's failure to seek clarification on the discrepancies, despite having prior knowledge of them, reflected negligence in the investigation and undermined the credibility of the prosecution's case.
The court ordered that the accused be acquitted and discharged of all three charges without being called to enter his defence, and that his bail money be refunded.
A total of 18 prosecution witnesses testified during the trial.
Saharulzaman, who was attached to the Commercial Crime Investigation Division at the Lahad Datu district police headquarters at the time of the alleged offences, claimed trial when the charges were first read to him at the Tawau Sessions Court on July 8, 2022.
According to the charges, he was accused of soliciting a RM20,000 bribe as an inducement not to take action against an individual suspected of operating an unlicensed moneylending business, as well as requesting RM4,000 as protection money for the activity.
He was also charged with accepting RM17,000 from the same individual inside a Perodua Alza at Double Bay, Lahad Datu, on Aug 16, 2017.
For the two charges of soliciting bribes, he was charged under Section 16(a)(B) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009, while the charge of accepting a bribe was framed under Section 17(a) of the same Act.
The prosecution was led by Sabah MACC deputy public prosecutor Wardah Ishhar, while the accused was represented by lawyer Mohamed Zairi Zainal Abidin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Forestry authorities detect 21 firms for illegal logging in protected forests
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Forestry authorities have detected 21 logging companies for encroaching into Permanent Reserved Forests nationwide since last year, with most cases linked to organised syndicates.
Peninsular Malaysia Forestry Department director-general Datuk Zahari Ibrahim said 17 companies were identified in 2025, while four more were detected between January and March this year.
He said investigations and field analyses showed most large-scale forest reserve encroachment cases, particularly illegal logging, involved organised syndicates rather than individuals.
Zahari said evidence included the use of heavy machinery such as excavators, bulldozers and lorries, indicating significant financial backing.
He said syndicates also recruited foreign workers from various countries to carry out encroachment activities on the ground.
Zahari said enforcement capabilities had been strengthened over the past two years through integrated monitoring systems capable of detecting encroachment and illegal logging in real time.
He said among the key tools deployed were satellite-based monitoring systems to track changes in forest cover, alongside the Forest Monitoring Using Remote Sensing system developed with the Malaysian Space Agency.
[[nid:1461656]]
He said the department also used drones equipped with thermal imaging for night surveillance in forested areas.
"We have enhanced our forest enforcement capabilities over the past two years through integrated monitoring systems, including satellite technology, remote sensing systems developed with MYSA and drone surveillance to detect encroachment and illegal logging activities in real time," he said.
Describing the issue as increasingly serious, Zahari said forest reserve encroachment could be measured through its economic impact, frequency and environmental damage.
He said forest produce worth RM3.55 million had been seized and saved by enforcement authorities over a 15-month period from 2025 to March this year.
He said encroachment extended beyond timber theft to include land clearing for agriculture such as oil palm, vegetables and ginger, illegal settlements and sand mining.
Zahari warned such activities posed long-term environmental risks, including soil erosion, river pollution and biodiversity loss.
He said encroachment into permanent forest reserves was no longer petty theft but organised environmental crime driven by greed and profit.
He said the Forestry Department was strengthening cooperation with enforcement agencies to track down those involved.
He added that action under the National Forestry Act 1984 would be taken without compromise.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Second day of firefighting as dump blaze rages near IIUM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Firefighters are continuing efforts for a second day to extinguish a large rubbish dump fire near the International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM), with about half of the blaze brought under control as of this afternoon.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the fire, which involved a rubbish heap, was still actively burning despite continuous firefighting operations since yesterday.
"As of 2.40pm today, firefighters have successfully extinguished about 50 per cent of the fire, while the remaining 50 per cent is still being tackled.
"Operations are being carried out using an 800ft (152.4m) relay pumping system with three water jets, assisted by an excavator provided by the Selayang Municipal Council (MPS)," he said.
The department received an emergency call on the incident at 10.59am yesterday and dispatched a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) vehicle from the Selayang Fire and Rescue Station.
The first fire engine arrived at the scene at 11.12am, with seven firefighters led by operations commander PBK I Suhaimi Samoori deployed to contain the blaze.
Mukhlis said firefighting operations were suspended at 6.31pm yesterday due to operational considerations before resuming at 8.40am today.
He said six personnel from the Gombak Volunteer Fire Brigade, four officers from MPS and two officers from the district health office were also assisting at the scene.
No casualties have been reported.
Photographs from the scene showed thick white smoke blanketing the hillside rubbish dump as firefighters worked along steep embankments to contain hotspots.
Several personnel were seen directing powerful water jets into smouldering waste while an excavator moved debris and earth to expose hidden pockets of fire beneath the rubbish.
The blaze also generated a dense haze over the surrounding vegetation, with firefighters forced to operate in challenging conditions amid smoke-filled surroundings and rugged terrain.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspect in Kampung Gajah murder and rape case arrested in Perlis
Article:
IPOH: A man suspected of fatally stabbing another man and raping his ex-girlfriend at Km12, Jalan Trans Perak, near Kampung Gajah, was arrested in Perlis.
Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the 30-year-old was detained at Perlis Sentral Bus Terminal in Kangar, about 3.45pm by a team from the Perak and Perlis Criminal Investigation Departments.
He said police also recovered a knife that the suspect had disposed of in a rubbish bin in Kangar town.
"The suspect met his former girlfriend, aged 25, to discuss relationship issues about 9am on Tuesday.
"During the encounter in a padi field area in Jalan Trans Perak, the suspect allegedly threatened and raped the woman in his car," he said.
Alwi was speaking to reporters after witnessing the handing over of duties ceremony for the Perak Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) chief at the Senior Police Officers' Mess in Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah.
He said, after raping his former grilfriend, he dropped the woman off about 5am on Wednesday at her car.
The woman's current boyfriend then arrived at the scene and the suspect fled in his car.
Alwi said the boyfriend chased the suspect and managed to ram and block the man's vehicle.
At this point, he said, the suspect attacked the boyfriend and stabbed the victim in the abdomen.
"The suspect then fled in the victim's car before abandoning it in Persiaran Batu Gajah Perdana in Batu Gajah.
"With the suspect's arrest, the murder and rape cases were solved in less than 24 hours. The suspect has been remanded for seven days from today to assist investigations under Section 302 (murder) and Section 376 (rape) of the Penal Code," he said.
The man, if found guilty for murder, can be sentenced to death or be put in jail for between 30 and 40 years, with whipping of no less than 12 strokes of the rotan.
If convicted of rape, he can be sentenced to up to 20 years in jail.
Yesterday, Perak Tengah police chief Superintendent Hafezul Helmi Hamzah said a 29-year-old man died from severe stab wounds sustained during a fight with the another man on Wednesday.
He had said the victim was taken to a clinic for treatment before being pronounced dead by a medical officer.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to take action against Rohingya refugees violating laws, says PM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government will take action against Rohingya refugees found violating the country's laws and regulations, including matters involving businesses and the construction of buildings.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the issue of the increasing Rohingya population in Malaysia was discussed with Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail during today's Cabinet meeting.
"My advice to Myanmar nationals, particularly the Rohingya, is that they must comply with the regulations in our country. Their buildings and businesses must follow the rules.
"We will also discuss this with state governments as matters involving buildings and urban development fall under their jurisdiction, and they too must take firm action," he said after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Al-Hidayah here today.
On the repatriation of Rohingya refugees in Malaysia, Anwar said the matter involved humanitarian considerations.
He said the Myanmar government was currently preventing the return of Rohingya refugees to the country.
"Where can we send them? We have a problem with the Myanmar government, which is preventing repatriation.
"We have also yet to find a solution to send them to a third country or return them home. It is not only us; the entire region, including Bangladesh, is facing this issue, with millions of refugees, including those in Cox's Bazar," he said.
Meanwhile, Anwar urged Malaysians, especially Muslims, not to take matters into their own hands.
"I hope our people, especially the Muslim community, will not break the law in dealing with this issue," he said.
This comes following the discovery of a 0.4-hectare Rohingya settlement hidden on private land near Jalan Sungai Tekali in Hulu Langat, which is believed to have existed for nearly 10 years.
The site is located about one kilometre from another viral settlement where Rohingya residents allegedly built unauthorised four-storey structures.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thailand declares 15-day national mourning for Princess Bajrakitiyabha
Article:
BANGKOK: Thailand on Friday declared a 15-day period of national mourning following the death of Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendira Debyavati, the eldest child of King Maha Vajiralongkorn, with flags to be flown at half-mast at government institutions nationwide.
Government Spokesperson Ratchada Thanadirek said the national mourning arrangements were endorsed at a special Cabinet meeting after ministers were informed of the Princess's death by the Bureau of the Royal Household.
"The Cabinet has directed civil servants, state enterprise employees and government officials to observe the mourning period, while encouraging members of the public to do likewise," she said in a statement.
Ratchada said Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul paid tribute to the Princess and led Cabinet ministers in observing a moment of silence to honour her contributions to the nation.
She said the national mourning period took effect immediately, with Anutin directing all relevant agencies to ensure the royal funeral ceremonies are conducted with full honours and in accordance with royal traditions.
Ratchada said a committee chaired by Anutin would be established under the Prime Minister's Office to oversee all aspects of the funeral arrangements, including ceremonial administration, the construction of the royal crematorium, public communications and security.
She said the Culture Ministry, through the Fine Arts Department, would oversee ceremonial protocols and the construction of the royal crematorium, while the  Interior Ministry and Bangkok Metropolitan Administration would organise commemorative activities for the public to pay tribute to the late Princess.
The Bureau of the Royal Household announced on Friday that Princess Bajrakitiyabha
died
peacefully at Chulalongkorn Hospital at 7.48pm on Thursday while undergoing treatment. She was 47.
Princess Bajrakitiyabha had been receiving treatment at King Chulalongkorn Memorial Hospital since December 2022 after collapsing from a heart-related condition while training her pet dog in Pak Chong district, Nakhon Ratchasima province.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil extends losses as Trump calls off planned strikes on Iran
Article:
SINGAPORE: Oil prices fell more than two per cent on Friday, extending losses from the previous session after US President Donald Trump cancelled plans to strike Iran, reducing fears of an escalation of hostilities following tit-for-tat attacks earlier in the week.
Brent futures were down US$2.11 or 2.3 per cent at US$88.27 a barrel by 0640 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude dropped US$1.90, or 2.2 per cent to US$85.81.
Trump called off planned strikes on Thursday, saying discussions with Iran had progressed and a peace deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz to shipping could be signed as soon as this weekend, even as Tehran said it had not made a final decision.
"While this could, of course, be yet another false dawn, the market's reaction has been both swift and decisive," said IG market analyst Tony Sycamore.
He added that even as oil prices correct downwards, "as long as the price can hold above support in the low US$80s, the risks remain firmly skewed to the upside".
On Thursday, Iran announced "the closure" of the Strait of Hormuz, through which vessel traffic was already severely limited, saying it would fire on any ship trying to pass through the waterway.
The strait normally carries a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas shipments, and Tehran's months-long blockade has kept energy prices elevated.
State media reported on Friday that Iranian forces had prevented a tanker from transiting the Strait of Hormuz without coordination.
The US military said on social media that commercial ships continued to transit the waterway.
"We would be cautious about assuming that the extension of the ceasefire is a done deal. Even if it is, it could be fragile. And clearly, if nuclear talks do not progress, it could very easily fall apart," said ING analysts in a Friday note.
"We believe the market reaches an inflection point in late July if we do not see oil flows resuming before then. This is when inventory levels and seasonally stronger demand push prices significantly higher towards US$120-130 per barrel."
The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries on Thursday lowered its forecast for 2026 world oil demand growth to 970,000 barrels per day from a previous 1.17 million bpd, marking its second straight downward revision.
The producer group also said consumption would rebound later, raising its demand growth forecast for 2027. It expects 2027 oil demand to rise by 1.73 million bpd, up 190,000 bpd from its previous forecast.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fire partially destroys Gebeng waste plant, 200 students evacuated
Article:
KUANTAN: About 200 students from the Gebeng Advanced Technology Training Centre hostel were evacuated after a fire partially destroyed a waste management plant next to the hostel in the Gebeng industrial area today.
About 100 firefighters, including volunteers from the Gebeng Emergency Mutual Aid (Gema) unit and nearby factories, battled the blaze for almost two hours.
At the height of the
fire
during the morning rush hour, thick black smoke could be seen billowing  into the sky. Commuters stopped to record the scene on their mobile phones.
Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim said the department received a distress call at 6.29am, and firefighters from the Gebeng fire station arrived within 10 minutes.
"The fire is believed to have started at the processing facility before spreading to the storage area, fuelled by combustible materials.
"The processing facility, where oil-based waste is treated, was completely destroyed, while the storage facility was partially damaged. The fire was brought under control at 8.25am," he told reporters outside the plant today.
Razam said no casualties were reported as only a security guard was on the premises when the fire broke out. No workers were present.
He said the students were evacuated as a precaution due to the thick smoke and concerns over possible exposure to hazardous chemicals.
"Three ambulances were stationed at the site in case of smoke inhalation.
"The students were taken to a safe location while the Hazardous Materials (Hazmat) team checks out the facility.
"They will be allowed to return later today once the area is declared safe," he said.
He said the department's forensic team was investigating the cause of the fire and assessing the extent of the losses.
Razam said firefighters from the Gebeng, Kuantan, Peramu and Taman Tas fire stations, together with volunteer firefighters from several agencies, including the police and the Department of Environment, were assisting at the scene.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia leads in Islamic derivatives, but adoption lags
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia remains one of the world's most advanced jurisdictions for Islamic finance, offering both over-the-counter (OTC) and exchange-traded Islamic derivatives, according to Fitch Ratings.
However, the rating agency said conventional derivatives still dominate the market, and it estimates only one per cent of derivatives were Islamic in 2025.
According to Fitch, the heightened volatility in commodity prices, profit rates and exchange rates following the onset of the Iran war has underscored the growing importance of Islamic derivatives as a risk-management tool.
The rating agency said about 75 per cent of its rated Islamic banks used or offered Islamic derivatives in 2025 and the first quarter (Q1) 2026, with full adoption among rated Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Islamic banks.
These are primarily profit-rate swaps, forward foreign-exchange contracts, and cross-currency swaps and, in some cases, commodity hedging solutions.
"The remaining rated Islamic banks that did not actively deploy derivatives were mainly in Indonesia, Jordan, Iraq, Nigeria and Tunisia," it said.
Fitch said the majority of rated Islamic banks have deployed Islamic derivatives, but adoption is rare amongst rated Islamic insurance companies.
However, it said the Islamic derivatives market still lags conventional derivatives in most core Islamic finance markets.
"The conventional derivatives market is also underdeveloped across most OIC countries compared to other regions.
"The combined turnover of OTC interest-rate derivatives in Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain, Malaysia, Indonesia and Turkiye accounted for less than one per cent of global volumes in April 2025," it said.
Fitch said just under two thirds of  its rated Islamic banks were investment grade at end-1Q26, with around 80 per cent on stable outlooks.
It said most GCC Islamic banks have been resilient to the Iran war as they have sound financial metrics and ample liquidity and capital buffers.
"When Fitch is assessing a bank's risk profile, high exposures to structural or traded market risks, which are weakly mitigated or managed, would be deemed negative.
"In contrast, moderate and well-managed exposure to structural or traded market risks would be deemed positive," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rohingya labourer claims trial to robbing badminton legend Razif Sidek at knifepoint
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Rohingya labourer pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to robbing national badminton legend Datuk Mohamad Razif Mohd Sidek at knifepoint of RM60,000 worth of belongings last month.
Mohamad Fawyas Yasin, 27, was accused of committing the offence while armed with a knife at 12.20pm at Razif's residence along Jalan Damansara, Taman Tun Dr Ismail on May 19.
The charge under Section 392 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 397, carries a maximum jail term of 14 years, a fine or whipping upon conviction.
The accused entered his plea before Sessions Court judge Siti Shakirah Mohtaruddin.
Deputy public prosecutor J. Banusha objected to bail due to the seriousness of the offence and concerns over the accused's whereabouts.
She informed the court that the accused's United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card had expired.
"If bail is granted and the accused fails to attend court proceedings, it would be difficult to trace him as he does not have a fixed address.
"There is also a risk because the accused knows the victim's address and could potentially harass him," she said.
Defence lawyer Mansheel Kaur urged the court to grant bail at a minimal sum as her client earns only RM50 a day and is the sole breadwinner for his unemployed wife and their 10-month-old child.
She added that the accused was in the process of renewing his UNHCR card.
However, Siti Shakirah denied bail on the grounds that there was no valid UNHCR card before the court for consideration and fixed July 14 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Global tech giants Agibot and Huawei enter Johor
Article:
KULAI: Johor has officially positioned itself as a premier regional force in high-tech investment, securing partnerships with global technology titans, including Huawei and Agibot, to anchor the landmark RM80 billion Johor Tech Smart City project.
This high-impact project, valued at RM80 billion and spanning 930.78 hectares, serves as a strategic move by the state government to accelerate its digital agenda and food security in line with the Maju Johor 2030 aspirations.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi stated that the project is more than a physical development; it is a catalyst for Johor's economic transformation and is projected to create over 10,000 quality job opportunities.
"This initiative focuses on sectors including technology, artificial intelligence (AI), smart agriculture, and talent research and development (R&D). We want to ensure that economic prosperity directly benefits the people, particularly through local talent development," he said in his opening remarks at the Agtech City programme in Bandar Genting here, today.
To streamline investment, the state government is committed to facilitating business through one-stop centres like the Invest Malaysia Facilitation Centre Johor, aimed at reducing bureaucracy.
Johor Tech Smart City will be developed as an integrated ecosystem combining an AgTech Campus and a Knowledge AI Campus. To realise this vision, Genting Property and ACGT Sdn Bhd are collaborating with a network of global technical leaders.
Huawei's world-renowned smart campuses in Shanghai and Dongguan serve as the foundational inspiration for the Knowledge AI Campus master plan, supported by urban planning and smart systems design experts B+H and Surbana Jurong.
The AgTech Campus ecosystem is reinforced by industry pioneers, including China Shouguang Vegetable Industry Group for seed R&D and tropical crop development, DAYU Irrigation for advanced precision water and nutrient management, XAG for market-leading autonomous agri-robotics, and AIRSAT, which delivers satellite intelligence and remote crop data analytics.
Onn Hafiz emphasised that the development would ensure that local Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) are integrated into the supply chain, preventing them from being left behind in this new economic landscape.
"We must ensure that the SMEs in Johor must also prosper as companies from China enter. While we welcome the Chinese companies entering Johor, our SMEs should not be left behind," he said.
This project is part of Johor's strong economic growth momentum, with the state recording RM110 billion in approved investments over the past two years since January 2024, setting a national record.
These efforts are expected to establish Johor as a top destination for global investors in innovation, future technology, and food security in Southeast Asia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A year after deadly Air India crash, families await answers
Article:
AHMEDABAD: Families of those killed in last year's Air India plane crash gathered at the site on Friday to mark the anniversary of the disaster, still awaiting answers about its cause.
On June 12, 2025, a Boeing 787 crashed into a medical college shortly after take-off in India's western city of Ahmedabad, killing 260 people in the deadliest air disaster in a decade.
Indian authorities are expected to issue an interim report in the coming days, a source of frustration for the victims' relatives, who had been expecting a final disclosure.
Suresh Patni, a driver, came to the site where the plane exploded in a ball of flames, engulfing his teenage son Akash at the family's tea stall.
"We are here today only to remember him on his first death anniversary," Patni told AFP.
"He was a good student and could have done really well for himself."
Patni commemorated Akash with a framed photograph and a life-size cut-out, decorated with flowers and surrounded by scattered rose petals and lit lamps.
The
crash
killed 241 people on board the plane and 19 on the ground.
Fragments of bags, clothes and a melted shoe lie half-buried in the charred earth at the site, alongside dead trees with burned trunks.
"It pains us when we hear an aeroplane flying overhead," Patni said, adding that their home is near the airport's flight path in Ahmedabad, the main city in Gujarat.
"Our house is still at the same location," he said. "But we don't feel like staying here… we are reminded of the same faces and memories."
'Important for closure'
Nearby, a woman wept as she embraced a framed picture of her deceased relatives, while another family scattered rose petals at the ruins of the hostel in honour of their son.
Relatives of the victims had expected a final report by Friday to explain why the disaster happened.
But with investigations continuing, the Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau (AAIB) is expected to issue only an interim report.
As required by international law, the AAIB published a preliminary
report
a month after the disaster.
The 15-page document said the fuel supply to the jet's engines was cut off moments before impact, raising questions about possible pilot error.
It also published a conversation between the captain and co-pilot about the fuel supply being cut off — two brief sentences that sparked theories of pilot suicide.
The report was met with strong criticism.
It did not explain why the fuel switches were turned off, whether due to pilot error or a malfunction.
Relatives of the victims are meeting at a conference organised by lawyers, along with aviation and air safety experts in Ahmedabad.
They are due to hold a candlelight vigil after sunset.
"Why are authorities taking so much time to assess the crash?" asked Nilesh Joshi, whose wife, Kaminiben Nilesh Joshi, was killed while returning home to Britain after attending a wedding in India.
"The release of the report is important for closure, for people like me who have lost their loved ones," Joshi, who came from London to attend the conference, told AFP.
Only one passenger survived - Briton Vishwash Kumar Ramesh, who said he has "significant psychological scars" following the crash, in which his brother died, and "constant unanswered questions" about why it happened.
Vijay Sengal still remembers the deafening sound when the plane came down.
Sengal, a sanitation inspector at a nearby hospital, was among the first to try to rescue the injured.
"When we tried to pick up bodies, the body wouldn't come… instead, it was someone's hand, someone's leg," he said.
He added that he, like many others, avoids the area after dark, fearing it is haunted.
"We believe in gods and also in souls," he said. "Those passengers sitting in the plane, maybe they still have some work left undone, their last wish unfulfilled."
- AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorcyclist who slashed rider with meat cleaver remanded for four days
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A motorcyclist who allegedly attacked another rider with a meat cleaver on the Federal Highway motorcycle lane yesterday has been remanded for four days.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the 43-year-old man was detained for investigations under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt with a dangerous weapon.
"The suspect has been remanded for four days for further investigation, while the 45-year-old victim is still receiving treatment at Shah Alam Hospital and is reported to be in stable condition," he said today.
It was reported yesterday that a motorcyclist was injured after being slashed by another rider on the Federal Highway motorcycle lane near Section 13 early yesterday morning.
The victim
suffered injuries
to the back of his neck, arm and hand.
Sarudin said police received an emergency call through the MERS 999 hotline at 8.19am regarding the incident.
Initial investigations found that the incident occurred at 7am when the 45-year-old victim was riding to work in Kuala Lumpur.
"While using the lane, the victim honked at another motorcyclist who was blocking his path.
"The suspect, who was dissatisfied with the action, then stopped by the roadside and slashed the victim with a meat cleaver," he said in a statement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas to decide Bersatu's status in PN this Wednesday
Article:
MARANG: Pas will decide on Bersatu's position within Perikatan Nasional at the party's central working committee meeting this Wednesday,
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said the removal of any party from PN depended on the majority decision of the coalition's component members.
He said PN comprised four component parties, namely Pas as the largest party in the coalition, Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party.
"Based on the PN constitution, the removal of any component party requires a majority decision by the member parties," he said after delivering a Friday sermon at Masjid Rusila here today.
Hadi, who is also the Marang member of parliament, said this when commenting on the current situation within PN.
It is understood that several top PN leaders said a meeting between Pas and the two component parties, Gerakan and MIPP, was held yesterday to discuss the latest status.
Hadi said Pas held the PN chairmanship and Bersatu was still a member, alongside Gerakan and MIPP.
"It is up to those two parties whether to support Bersatu or Pas, but based on the constitution, the chairman has powers, including determining election logos and other matters.
"The previous PN chairman made that ruling to bind us, which is why we want to break free from that," he said.
He added that Pas would leave it to Gerakan and MIPP to decide whether to support Pas or Bersatu in determining the coalition's future direction, following the end of political cooperation between Pas and Bersatu last Monday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's factory output posts fastest growth since September 2022
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's Industrial Production Index (IPI) expanded at its fastest pace since September 2022 in April, rising 8.2 per cent year-on-year as manufacturing, mining and electricity output all recorded stronger growth.
The performance marked a sharp acceleration from the 3.1 per cent increase recorded in March, according to the Statistics Department.
Chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said manufacturing remained the main growth engine, with output climbing 8.3 per cent compared with 5.5 per cent in the previous month, underscoring the broad-based strength of the industrial sector.
The mining sector returned to positive territory, expanding 6.8 per cent after contracting 6.5 per cent in March, while electricity generation increased 10.5 per cent, accelerating from 4.8 per cent previously.
Despite the strong annual performance, the IPI declined 3.4 per cent month-on-month in April after registering a 9.3 per cent increase in March.
The monthly decline was mainly attributed to lower output in the manufacturing and mining sectors, which contracted 4.4 per cent and 1.6 per cent respectively.
Electricity output, however, rose 2.1 per cent during the month.
Export-oriented industries, which account for about two-thirds of the manufacturing sector, expanded 8.5 per cent YoY in April, up from 6.7 per cent in March.
The growth was supported by a 14.6 per cent increase in the manufacture of computer, electronic and optical products, alongside gains in the manufacture of vegetable and animal oils and fats (9.3 per cent) and coke and refined petroleum products (4.2 per cent).
The department said the stronger performance was in line with Malaysia's manufactured exports, which increased 11.7 per cent in April.
On a monthly basis, however, export-oriented industries declined 8.7 per cent after recording an 11.9 per cent increase in March.
Domestic-oriented industries also posted stronger growth, rising 8.0 per cent compared with 2.8 per cent in the previous month.
The expansion was led by the manufacture of motor vehicles, trailers and semi-trailers, which surged 13.5 per cent, as well as food processing products, which grew 8.6 per cent.
Manufacturing activities linked to the construction sector also recorded gains, including fabricated metal products, except machinery and equipment, which rose 5.8 per cent, and other non-metallic mineral products, up 6.7 per cent.
Month-on-month, domestic-oriented industries increased 5.3 per cent, improving from 1.4 per cent growth in March.
In the mining sector, the turnaround was largely driven by a 16.6 per cent increase in natural gas production, compared with a 3.4 per cent decline in March.
Production of crude oil and condensate, however, continued to contract, falling 6.4 per cent YoY, although the decline was less severe than the 11.3 per cent drop recorded a month earlier.
Meanwhile, electricity generation expanded 10.5 per cent YoY and rose 2.1 per cent from March.
Compared with regional and global peers, Singapore recorded the highest industrial production growth in April at 17.6 per cent, followed by Taiwan at 14.2 per cent, Vietnam at 9.3 per cent and Malaysia at 8.2 per cent.
Industrial output also increased in China (4.1 per cent), Japan (2.3 per cent), South Korea (1.5 per cent) and the US (1.4 per cent), while Thailand registered a 0.4 per cent contraction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Education Ministry to probe alleged Rohingya community school in Terengganu
Article:
IPOH: The establishment of schools in the country is subject to the provisions of the Education Act and cannot operate without official approval.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the Education Ministry will investigate claims of a Rohingya community school operating in Kampung Ladang Titian, Kuala Terengganu, before taking further action.
"All schools are subject to the Education Act, and absolutely none can be established without approval.
"We will monitor the situation in Terengganu and take appropriate action," he said after attending the Sekolah Menengah Jenis Kebangsaan Ave Maria Convent academic excellence ceremony here today.
Wong was responding to media reports yesterday that a suspected Rohingya community school had sparked concern and unease among Kuala Terengganu residents since February.
He said the ministry constantly monitors compliance to ensure all educational institutions operate within the law.
Earlier, Wong congratulated 75 of the school's students for excellent 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia examination results, including 37 who achieved 10 to 12 As.
He said the results reflect the commitment of teachers, the school board, the Parent-Teacher Association and students to raising academic standards.
At the event, he also announced a RM50,000 special allocation from his office for student-led projects at the school.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysians advised to avoid Central Jakarta demonstration areas
Article:
JAKARTA: The Malaysian Embassy here has advised Malaysians in the capital to avoid demonstration hotspots in Central Jakarta today and closely follow updates from local authorities.
In an advisory today, the embassy said it was monitoring reports of demonstrations, particularly around Bundaran HI and the Arjuna Wiwaha Statue, also known as Patung Kuda, where traffic disruptions were expected throughout the day.
The embassy said the protests would cause traffic disruptions in several parts of Central Jakarta, particularly along Jalan MH Thamrin, Jalan Jenderal Sudirman and Jalan Medan Merdeka Barat.
"Malaysians are strongly advised to avoid demonstration areas and refrain from engaging in any activities that may violate local laws and regulations," it said.
The embassy urged Malaysians requiring consular assistance to contact its hotline at +62 813-8081-3036.
Local media reported that student groups organised the protests over rising living costs, fuel prices and government policies.
Meanwhile, the Jakarta Metropolitan Police deployed 6,088 police and military personnel to secure several protest locations, including the House of Representatives complex, Bundaran HI, Patung Kuda and Cikini Raya.
Police spokesperson Budi Hermanto said the deployment was intended to ensure the protests could proceed safely and orderly while safeguarding public activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KL police to double presence in 122 schools to combat illegal racing, traffic offences
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police will double their presence in 122 secondary schools here under a new traffic safety advocacy programme aimed at educating students on road safety and illegal street racing.
Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the Traffic Awareness Advocacy Programme, which began today, would also involve other police departments to strengthen crime prevention awareness among students.
"Road safety is not merely about complying with laws. It is a moral and social responsibility that must be shouldered by every road user.
"Every day, thousands of vehicles use Kuala Lumpur's road network, which serves as the backbone of economic activity and daily life," he said at the launch of the programme at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Alam Damai in Cheras today.
Also present were Federal Territory Education Department Planning and Management Sector deputy director Aziz Hashim, Kuala Lumpur Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department chief Senior Assistant Commissioner Ravinder Singh, and Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa.
Fadil said police records showed that several students had been detained for traffic-related offences, with some involved in illegal street racing.
He said the programme was specifically targeted at school students to ensure teenagers understood the importance of complying with traffic laws from an early age.
"We want them to first understand and comply with basic traffic regulations before they move on to understanding and respecting other laws in society. That is why schools are our primary target.
"We want students to know what is right and what is wrong. They must understand that offences such as illegal racing carry serious consequences," he said.
Fadil added that continuous engagement with students was crucial in cultivating a culture of responsible road use and reducing the involvement of youths in dangerous activities on the roads.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kuala Lumpur police launch probe into station chief over corruption claims [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have opened an investigation into allegations of corruption and abuse of power involving a station chief under the Wangsa Maju district police headquarters.
Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said an investigation paper had been initiated to determine whether the allegations were substantiated or baseless.
He said the district police chief and the Integrity and Standards Compliance Department had been instructed to carry out the investigation.
"At this stage, investigations are ongoing," he said after launching the Kuala Lumpur Police Traffic Awareness Advocacy Programme at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Alam Damai in Cheras today.
The investigation aims to determine whether the allegations are supported by facts, he added.
Fadil said the officer implicated had had his statement recorded and remained on duty pending the outcome of the probe.
He said appropriate action would be taken if any offence was established.
Earlier, a screenshot circulating on social media alleged that an inspector had instructed subordinates to collect protection money from gambling outlets and massage parlours operating in Wangsa Maju.
The post also accused the officer of directing personnel to extort foreign nationals and of bringing female attendants into the police station.
Fadil said police would conduct a thorough investigation to establish the facts before deciding on further action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Unilateral child conversion laws in six states stand as High Court dismisses challenge by Indira Gandhi, 13 others
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The High Court today dismissed a challenge by Ipoh mother M. Indira Gandhi and 13 others against state laws that allow children under age 18 to be converted to Islam without both parents’ consent.
Court of Appeal judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman, who was a High Court judge when this case was heard, said that all 14 of them had failed to show they have
locus standi,
meaning the legal standing, to pursue this court challenge.
The judge also said they had failed to show that they have a “real or genuine interest”.
But as this court challenge was filed as a public interest matter, the judge did not order Indira and the 13 others to pay for legal costs.
Indira and the 13 others had wanted the courts to declare that the practice of unilateral conversion is unconstitutional, and to declare that provisions in several state laws allowing unilateral conversion to Islam are invalid or have no legal effect.
To be precise, their challenge were against provisions in six state laws in six states (Perlis, Kedah, Melaka, Negeri Sembilan, Perak and Johor) and a similar law for the Federal Territories (Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and Labuan), which allowed those under age 18 to be converted to Islam with just one parent’s consent.
MORE TO COME

Title: Two small parties, one big decision: Hadi puts Gerakan and MIPP at centre of Perikatan power play
Article:
MARANG, June 12 — PAS will decide on Bersatu’s future in Perikatan Nasional (PN) at its central working committee meeting next Wednesday, party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed today, days after political cooperation between the coalition’s two biggest parties collapsed.
Hadi said any expulsion from PN must be backed by a majority of the coalition’s four component parties: PAS, Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP).
“Based on the PN constitution, the removal of any component party requires a majority decision by the member parties,” he was quoted by the
New Straits Times
as telling reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Rusila here this afternoon.
Hadi said PAS would leave it to both parties to choose between PAS and Bersatu in charting the coalition’s direction.
According to the news report, several top PN leaders met representatives of Gerakan and MIPP yesterday to discuss the coalition’s latest status.
Hadi maintained that PAS now holds the PN chairmanship, with Bersatu remaining a member for the time being – but signalled the party wants to undo decisions made under the previous chairman, Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.
“It is up to those two parties whether to support Bersatu or PAS, but based on the constitution, the chairman has powers, including determining election logos and other matters.
“The previous PN chairman made that ruling to bind us, which is why we want to break free from that,” the Marang MP was quoted as saying.
PAS announced the end of its political cooperation with Bersatu last Monday, throwing the future of the opposition coalition into doubt ahead of the next general election.

Title: Police arrest 12, including 10 Chinese nationals, in Melaka scam call centre bust
Article:
MELAKA, June 12 — The police dismantled a ‘call centre’ syndicate suspected of being involved in a non-existent online investment scam with the arrest of 12 individuals in two raids at Taman Krubong Perdana here early on Wednesday morning.
Acting Melaka Tengah district police chief Supt Halim Abas said that those detained comprised 10 male Chinese nationals, in addition to a local man and woman, all aged between 23 and 47 years.
“In the first raid at 2.30 am, a team from the Melaka Commercial Crime Investigation Department (JSJK) arrested six individuals, consisting of five male Chinese nationals and one local man, all aged between 23 and 43 years.
“Meanwhile, the second raid at 3 am led to the arrest of five more male Chinese nationals and a local woman, all aged between 27 and 47 years,” he said at a press conference at the Melaka contingent police headquarters (IPK) here today.
Commenting further, Halim said that the results of the social media analysis experts’ review found that both syndicates engaged in online investment fraud activities that do not exist and are suspected of targeting Chinese citizens through digital platforms.
It was said that the investigation found that both syndicates had started operating since early June and were using the MiChat application to communicate with the victims.
The investigation results also found that all the suspects acted as customer service representatives who contacted target customers and provided explanations about online investments that could be accessed through the given link.
“Interested victims would choose the offered investment and make payments managed by other syndicate members who are still at large,” he said.
Halim said that in the raid, the police also seized 12 sets of monitors, mouses and keyboards, 12 CPU units, 12 mobile phones, as well as two sets of modems and routers worth approximately RM40,000, which were believed to be used for conducting the activities.
He said the investigation was conducted under Section 420 of the Penal Code read together with Section 120B of the Penal Code and Section 6(1)© of the Immigration Act 1959/63, and all suspects have been remanded from June 10 to June 23.
He also advised the public to be more cautious before making any money transfer transactions and to check with the bank or police if they feel suspicious about any financial dealings.
“The public is also advised to check phone numbers and account numbers through the Semak Mule website before making any transactions, and to immediately contact the bank or the National Scam Response Centre (NSRC) at 997 if they fall victim to cyber fraud,” he added. — Bernama

Title: Kedah schoolgirl unable to sit or stand since classmate’s chair-pulling prank in February
Article:
PADANG BESAR, June 12 — A 12-year-old girl has allegedly been unable to attend school for nearly four months after sustaining injuries in a fall when the chair she was about to sit on was allegedly pulled away by a classmate during a lesson in February.
Her mother, Norasikin Hasin, 32, claimed that the Year Six pupil suffered a soft tissue injury to her lower back and was subsequently diagnosed with functional neurological disorder and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) following the incident on Feb 11.
She said the incident occurred during a teaching and learning session when her daughter attempted to sit down after greeting a teacher, but fell to the floor after her chair was allegedly pulled away by a female classmate.
“My daughter began complaining of back pain after returning home from her hostel on Feb 13 for the school holidays in conjunction with Ramadan, and I only learned about the incident when I took her to Tuanku Fauziah Hospital in Kangar for treatment
“Since then, my daughter has been admitted to hospital several times due to persistent pain and numbness,” she told reporters at her home in Chuping, near here.
Norasikin said her daughter remains on medical leave and is unable to move about actively as she used to, beside not able to stand or sit for prolonged periods and that her studies have also been affected.
She said the girl’s emotional condition had also deteriorated, as she was frequently observed to be staring into space, crying suddenly and speaking incoherently at times, as well as expressing her feelings through writings and drawings in a notebook, which she described as resembling a personal diary.
“Through the entries, my daughter shared many of her feelings, including that some friends no longer wanted to befriend her and had asked her to complete their schoolwork.
“What saddened me most was a drawing depicting an individual standing at the edge of a high cliff facing the moon, which appeared to reflect the emotional distress she has been experiencing,” she said.
She also expressed her hope that the relevant authorities would help secure justice for her daughter, whom she described as a pupil with strong academic potential.
Meanwhile, Padang Besar district police chief ACP Mohd Shokri Abdullah confirmed that police had received a report regarding the incident, which had been referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor’s Office after being investigated under Section 323 of the Penal Code. — Bernama

Title: Small portions, big problems — the incredible shrinking menu hits Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 13 — Malaysia’s cafe and restaurant operators are currently caught in a perfect storm, and the latest industry data suggests that the clouds are not clearing.
According to the latest report from Retail Group Malaysia (RGM), sales at cafes and restaurants plummeted by 4.6 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the same period last year.
This is a jarring reversal from the 3.1 per cent growth seen in the final quarter of 2025. Even more telling is the gap between hope and reality: as recently as March, F&B businesses had forecast a growth of 1.9 per cent for the quarter. Instead, performance crashed far below those optimistic projections.
The timing is also notable, occurring during a window that should have been a gold mine, a period encompassing Chinese New Year, Hari Raya Aidilfitri, and school holidays, all of which typically trigger a surge in dining-out spending.
While takeaway outlets, kiosks, and stalls showed slightly more resilience, they were not spared, with sales dipping 1.4 per cent over the same period.
The root of the crisis is a familiar but intensifying pressure: the soaring cost of doing business.
RGM directly links the downturn to the broader economic fallout from the Middle East conflict, which has sent fuel prices spiralling since February. With diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia jumping 45 per cent and unsubsidised RON95 climbing 39 per cent between March and June, the industry has been hit where it hurts most.
In F&B, nearly every single input is sensitive to fuel costs: from the transport of raw ingredients and packaging to staff commutes and delivery logistics. For many operators, the impact was immediate and severe.
A precarious balancing act
Operators are now trapped in a precarious balancing act with no easy exit. Some have been forced to hike menu prices to protect their margins, only to see footfall drop as diners become increasingly price-conscious.
Others have opted for “shrinkflation” — quietly reducing portion sizes or switching to cheaper ingredients to keep prices steady. It is a gamble that risks alienating loyal customers if the drop in quality becomes too obvious.
The human cost of this squeeze is already becoming visible on the streets. RGM reports that during the second quarter, several independent operators were forced to shut their doors entirely, while major chains declined to renew leases for underperforming outlets in shopping malls.
This wave of closures and consolidation is a grim signal that the struggle is moving beyond the balance sheet and into the real world of vacant storefronts.
Crucially, the people running these businesses see no immediate relief. RGM’s data reveals a bleak outlook: cafe and restaurant operators are forecasting a further decline of 2.3 per cent for the second quarter of 2026, while the takeaway and stall sector expects another drop of 0.8 per cent.
This pessimism is compounded by a broader inflationary tide. Consumer prices for “food away from home” rose 2.6 per cent in April alone, the sharpest increase in the entire F&B category.
With the Malaysian government revising its full-year inflation forecast upward to between 1.5 per cent and 2.5 per cent, operators are entering the second half of the year with costs rising faster than their revenue.
For the average Malaysian diner, the symptoms are already here: smaller portions, higher bills, or a noticeable dip in quality at their favourite spots.
For the industry, the report suggests the sector will be under a sustained siege, where survival now depends on how well they can navigate a cost environment that shows no sign of easing.

Title: Obey Malaysian regulations or action will be taken, Anwar tells Rohingya refugees
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded Rohingya refugees in the country to comply with local laws and regulations at all times or face firm action.
He said that although Malaysia adopts a humanitarian approach, the group remains subject to the country's legal system, including regulations governing the use of premises and the operation of businesses.
“My advice to Myanmar nationals, particularly the Rohingya, is that they must also comply with the regulations in our country. Their buildings and businesses must adhere to the rules. If they do not, we will have no choice but to take action,” he said.
The Prime Minister said this to reporters after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Al-Hidayah in Sentul here today. — Bernama
MORE TO COME

Title: Why Johor votes first: Election Commission cites Negeri Sembilan’s surprise dissolution
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — Logistical demands and preparations for election workers were among the main reasons Johor and Negeri Sembilan will not hold their state elections simultaneously, the Election Commission (EC) said today.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said Negeri Sembilan’s unexpected dissolution of its state assembly left insufficient time for preparations to be completed,
Harian Metro
reported this afternoon.
“(The Negeri Sembilan assembly) was dissolved suddenly. So the situation was not ready at all,” he was quoted telling reporters after announcing key dates for the two state elections.
“Negeri Sembilan only has the ballot boxes. So we need time for them (EC personnel) to really focus,” he added.
The explanation comes after the EC scheduled Johor and Negeri Sembilan to vote nearly three weeks apart despite expectations that both elections would be held concurrently.
Johor voters will head to the polls on July 11, with nomination day fixed for June 27 and early voting on July 7.
Negeri Sembilan will hold its election on August 1, with nominations on July 18 and early voting on July 28.
The commission estimates the combined cost of both state elections at RM167.4 million, comprising RM86.8 million for Johor and RM80.6 million for Negeri Sembilan.
